diffCoverage.notes: Event tagging is on types::IncomingMessage.account_id (stamped in the zalo layer); the ui store routes by it. No layer boundary moved this slice.

findings.evidence: IncomingMessage.account_id is the per-account tag (pure types layer, no zca-rust dep); the zalo listener stamps it with api.get_own_id() so each event is attributable to its account. The ui session store keys AccountData by account_id and ingest() routes by msg.accountId. This is the concrete realization of the planned AppEvent { account_id, payload } — a separate wrapper type was unnecessary. Forward-only layering intact.

summary: Architecture review: events are tagged at the source (IncomingMessage.account_id, stamped in zalo) and routed by the ui store keyed on account_id — the planned AppEvent design realized inline. No layer boundary changed. Approve.
